docs: Aspirin was approved by the FDA in 1950 for treating fever and pain.
Ibuprofen replaced Aspirin as the first-line treatment for fever in children in 1986 due to Aspirin's link to Reye's syndrome.
Aspirin is still recommended by cardiologists in 2023 for prevention of heart attacks in high-risk patients.
Metformin was introduced in 1957 as the primary drug for managing Type 2 Diabetes.
Insulin therapy has been used since 1921 when Banting and Best discovered insulin at the University of Toronto.
Ozempic (semaglutide) was approved by the FDA in 2017 for Type 2 Diabetes and gained approval for obesity treatment in 2021.
queries: What is the current first-line treatment for fever in children?
Which drug is used for Type 2 Diabetes management?
